items.title: Show & Tell
items.description: A starter mission for sharing student examples and discussing AI.
items.iconObject: A pixel-art mini stage with a screen, two chairs, and a small smiling chat bubble.
items.bestFor: Hosts who want an event that works for students at any level of AI familiarity. Strong for inspiring broader use, sharing real examples, and creating a comfortable room for questions and opinions.

runOfShow.step: Open with one strong student example.
Invite two or three students to share how they already use AI.
Demo a few practical workflows live.
Discuss one big question the examples raise.
Have everyone write down one thing they want to try.

items.title: Prompt-A-Thon
items.description: A hands-on mission where students test and remix workflows in real time.
items.iconObject: A pixel-art stack of prompt cards, a lightning bolt, and a small stopwatch.
items.bestFor: Hosts working with a community already exploring AI. Great for a hands-on activity where students work in teams, solve a real problem, and reflect on what they built.

runOfShow.step: Introduce the Lab and set the mission.
Show the problem, starter prompt, and what students are trying to improve.
Form teams, pick an approach, and get everyone into the workspace.
Give attendees work time to test, remix, and build their best solution.
Vote, give awards, and spotlight strong submissions.
Close with discussion: what worked, what changed, and what people would try next.

items.title: Builder Lab
items.description: A deeper mission for students ready to build something with AI agents.
items.iconObject: A pixel-art toolbox with a wrench, pencil, code block, and tiny green blocks.
